text: Morning team — quick reminder we're doing the planning sync at 2pm today. Bring whatever you've got on the Q3 priorities doc. :coffee:

replies.text: On it, will have a draft ready by then
Same. Can we also block 10 mins at the end for the onboarding feedback?

text: Anyone else getting a weird flash of unstyled content on the marketing page first load? Repro: clear cache, hit `/pricing`

replies.text: yeah saw that yesterday too. Filed it as a P2

text: Lunch order going in at 12:30 — anyone want anything from the place across the street? :hamburger:
Folks — the new pricing experiment goes live tomorrow at 9am. Heads up in case anyone sees weird metrics.

replies.text: Got it. I'll keep an eye on the conversion funnel

text: PSA: the staging DB will be down 7-7:30am tomorrow for a quick maintenance window. Won't affect prod.
Sharing the updated brand guidelines draft — feedback by Friday would be great
Great talk from the meetup last night, recording is up: <https://example.com/talks/scaling-postgres>
Heads up — I'll be out Friday afternoon. Will be back Monday. :palm_tree:
Reminder: please update your weekly status doc before EOD Friday. We use these to put together the Monday update.
Made a meme to celebrate hitting 1k users this week :tada:
New office plant has been named: *Photosynthia*. She's by the kitchen.
Quick question for anyone who's used `pyproject.toml`-based packaging recently — does it play well with editable installs in 3.12?

replies.text: Yeah, but make sure you've got `pip>=23.1` and the right build backend

text: Team offsite is locked in for the third week of next month — calendar invites will go out today.
If anyone's in the office Friday, I'm bringing donuts :doughnut:
Sharing the customer feedback summary from this week — overall positive, two pain points around onboarding and one around the export flow.

replies.text: The onboarding one matches what I saw in the usability tests
Let's add it to next week's priorities

text: DNS migration done. New domain is live, old one redirects. Holler if anything breaks.
Anyone got a recommendation for a decent time-series DB? Looking at TimescaleDB vs. InfluxDB.

replies.text: Timescale if you already know Postgres. Way less learning curve.
+1 to that. We use it for the metrics pipeline.

text: Welcome to *Devi*! She joins us as a senior engineer starting today. Looking forward to having her on the team :wave:

replies.text: Excited to be here! Thanks everyone

text: Quarterly survey results are in — happiness score up 12 points from last quarter. Full write-up in the docs.
Sneak peek of the redesigned dashboard. Still iterating but wanted to share early :art:
Just merged the long-running infra branch. ~30% reduction in p99 latency on the API. :rocket:
Just finished the board update. TL;DR: revenue on track, two new pilot customers signed, hiring open for one more engineer.
First week wrap-up: codebase is in a great shape, docs are surprisingly good, and I've already shipped one PR. Loving it.
Kicking off the planning cycle for next quarter. Theme: less features, more polish.
First-ever post in this channel. Welcome everyone — let's build something we're proud of :sparkles:
